title: Gréville-Hague

infobox.value: This [Arrondissement of Cherbourg](/wiki/Arrondissement_of_Cherbourg "Arrondissement of Cherbourg") geographical article is a [stub](/wiki/Wikipedia:Stub "Wikipedia:Stub"). You can help Wikipedia by [adding missing information](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Gr%C3%A9ville-Hague&action=edit).

(French pronunciation: [[ɡʁevil aɡ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is a former [commune](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France") in the [Manche](/wiki/Manche "Manche") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") in [Normandy](/wiki/Normandy_(administrative_region) "Normandy (administrative region)") in north-western [France](/wiki/France "France"). On 1 January 2017, it was merged into the new commune [La Hague](/wiki/La_Hague "La Hague").[[2]](#cite_note-2) A hamlet (Gruchy) of the village is the birthplace of [Jean-François Millet](/wiki/Jean-Fran%C3%A7ois_Millet "Jean-François Millet"), a notable Realist painter.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Several of his most important paintings depict local landscapes or rural labour.

["Populations de référence 2022"](https://www.insee.fr/fr/statistiques/fichier/8290591/ensemble.pdf) [Reference populations 2022] (PDF) (in French). [INSEE](/wiki/Institut_national_de_la_statistique_et_des_%C3%A9tudes_%C3%A9conomiques "Institut national de la statistique et des études économiques"). December 2024.

outgoing_links.target_title: Institut national de la statistique et des études économiques

**Gréville-Hague** (French pronunciation: [[ɡʁevil aɡ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French")) is a former [commune](/wiki/Communes_of_France "Communes of France") in the [Manche](/wiki/Manche "Manche") [department](/wiki/Departments_of_France "Departments of France") in [Normandy](/wiki/Normandy_(administrative_region) "Normandy (administrative region)") in north-western [France](/wiki/France "France"). On 1 January 2017, it was merged into the new commune [La Hague](/wiki/La_Hague "La Hague").[[2]](#cite_note-2) A hamlet (Gruchy) of the village is the birthplace of [Jean-François Millet](/wiki/Jean-Fran%C3%A7ois_Millet "Jean-François Millet"), a notable Realist painter.[[3]](#cite_note-3) Several of his most important paintings depict local landscapes or rural labour.

Location of a major World War II battery, as well as massive French fortifications (never completed) to protect the deep-water port of [Cherbourg](/wiki/Cherbourg "Cherbourg").
